name: Simon Amstell
blurb: Simon Amstell is an English comedian, writer and director who first became known presenting the Channel 4 pop show Popworld and hosting the BBC panel game Never Mind the Buzzcocks.
bio: Simon Amstell is an English comedian, writer and director who first became known presenting the Channel 4 pop show Popworld and hosting the BBC panel game Never Mind the Buzzcocks. His stand-up is confessional and introspective, drawing on anxiety, relationships, identity and self-examination. He has also written and directed the sitcom Grandmas House and the films Carnage and Benjamin.

timeline.ev: Begins presenting Popworld on Channel 4 with Miquita Oliver
Becomes permanent host of Never Mind the Buzzcocks
Grandmas House, which he co-wrote and starred in, airs on BBC Two
Records the stand-up special Numb for the BBC
Writes and directs the feature film Benjamin
Releases the stand-up special Set Free on Netflix

accolades: British Comedy Award for Best Comedy Entertainment Personality (2007)
Royal Television Society Award for Best Entertainment Performance (2007)
BAFTA nomination for Best Entertainment Performance (2008)

movies_tv.t: How to Talk to Girls at Parties
